title: St. Joseph Church, Belman
tagline: St. Joseph Church, Belman — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: St. Joseph Church, Belman is a much-loved spiritual town in Karnataka, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Joseph Church, Belman is a Roman Catholic church and Parish is located in Belmannu, Karkala taluk, Udupi district, Karnataka, India. Formerly part of the Diocese of Mangalore, it became part of the Diocese of Udupi after Udupi became a separate diocese on 15 October 2011. The parish is part of the Archdiocese of Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Belman, Karkal
topPlaces.description: Belmannu, also called Belman, is a small town in Karkala taluk of Udupi district. The place derived its name from the Kannada word Bellimannu which means silver (belli) soil (mannu).

topPlaces.name: Santhoor
topPlaces.description: Santhoor is a village in Udupi taluk, Karnataka state, India. This village comes under Mudrangadi Gram Panchayat, Kapu Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Santhoor Subramanya Temple
topPlaces.description: Santhoor Subramanya Temple is a Hindu temple located in the village of Santhoor near Padubidri, Karnataka. Here Kartikeya is worshipped as Subramanya, lord of all serpents.

topPlaces.name: Nandalike
topPlaces.description: Nandalike is a village in Karkala taluk in the Udupi District in Karnataka, India. It is approximately 16 km east of Padubidri and about 15 km from Karkala town.

topPlaces.name: Mundkur
topPlaces.description: Mundkur is a village in Karkala Taluk, Udupi district, Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Munder, Udupi
topPlaces.description: Munder is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Karkala taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Balkunje
topPlaces.description: Balkunje is a panchayat town located at Mangalore taluk in Dakshina Kannada district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is on the banks of Shambhavi River.

topPlaces.name: Palimar
topPlaces.description: Palimar is a village and Grama Panchayat in the Udupi district of Karnataka state, India. Palimaru village is located on north banks of River Shambavi It houses Palimar Matha, one of the Ashta Mathas of Udupi founded by the Dvaita philosopher Madhvacharya.

items.note: Belmannu, also called Belman, is a small town in Karkala taluk of Udupi district. The place derived its name from the Kannada word Bellimannu which means silver (belli) soil (mannu).

Palimar is a village and Grama Panchayat in the Udupi district of Karnataka state, India. Palimaru village is located on north banks of River Shambavi It houses Palimar Matha, one of the Ashta Mathas of Udupi founded by the Dvaita philosopher Madhvacharya.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Joseph Church, Belman?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Joseph Church, Belman is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Joseph Church, Belman?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (23 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (34 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Joseph Church, Belman?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Joseph Church, Belman at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Joseph Church, Belman?
faq.a: Stays in St. Joseph Church, Belman start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Joseph Church, Belman?
faq.a: The top attractions are Belman, Karkal, Santhoor, Santhoor Subramanya Temple, Nandalike, Mundkur.
